seo_title: LLM Confidence Calibration: A Key to Improved Performance
meta_description: Large language models' confidence exceeds accuracy on average, but a new test can help improve performance.
body_html:
The current crop of LLMs are, like people, too sure they are right: confidence exceeds accuracy, on average.

Overconfidence is greatest on difficult tests; Easy tests actually show substantial underconfidence; The hard-easy effect moderates overconfidence in LLMs.

LifeEval: A Test for Model Calibration

Difficulty Level Accuracy Confidence

Benchmarks for LLM Calibration LifeEval, a test for evaluating model calibration across levels of difficulty, has shown promising results in improving LLM performance.

source.title: Confidence Calibration in Large Language Models
source.summary: Researchers have found that large language models (LLMs) tend to be overly confident in their responses, with confidence exceeding accuracy on average. However, this overconfidence is not uniform and varies depending on the level of difficulty, with LLMs being most overconfident on challenging tests. A new test called LifeEval has been developed to evaluate model calibration across different levels of difficulty.
